task_description:
Screen each abstract for relevance to machine learning for predicting patient outcomes in oncology (survival, recurrence, treatment response). Rate methodological quality.

Relevance: RELEVANT if the paper applies ML to predict any oncological patient outcome (survival time, recurrence, treatment response) from clinical, imaging, genomic, or pathology data. NOT_RELEVANT if the ML task is diagnosis, screening, drug discovery, or unrelated to oncology outcomes.

Quality rubric:
  1 = No methodology described or purely conceptual.
  2 = Approach mentioned but metrics vague or dataset underspecified.
  3 = Clear methodology, evaluation metrics, and dataset; limited comparison.
  4 = Rigorous evaluation with multiple baselines, external validation, and reproducibility.

papers.title: Neural Survival Model for Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Recurrence from CT Radiomics
papers.abstract: Post-resection recurrence prediction guides adjuvant therapy decisions. We extract 107 radiomic features from pre-surgery CT scans of 680 NSCLC patients and train a DeepHit neural survival model. C-index 0.72 is achieved on a held-out test set. We compare against Cox proportional hazards with the same features and an RSF baseline. An external validation cohort confirms generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: neural survival model, NSCLC, CT radiomics, recurrence prediction, DeepHit
papers.methodology_hint: DeepHit, 680 NSCLC, C-index 0.72, Cox and RSF baselines, external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: DeepHit for NSCLC recurrence prediction, C-index 0.72, 2 baselines, external validation.

papers.title: GAN-Based Data Augmentation for Rare Tumour Classification in Histopathology
papers.abstract: Rare sarcoma subtypes have few labelled pathology images. We train a conditional GAN to synthesise H&E images of three rare sarcoma subtypes and use the augmented data to train a CNN classifier. The classifier achieves macro-F1 0.73 compared to 0.61 without augmentation. This work is about diagnostic classification of tumour subtype, not patient outcome prediction.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, data augmentation, sarcoma, histopathology, classification
papers.methodology_hint: conditional GAN, H&E synthesis, macro-F1 0.73 — diagnostic classification not outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: GAN augmentation improving sarcoma classification macro-F1 to 0.73, diagnostic task not prognosis.

papers.title: AI for Oncology: A Broad Overview of Potential Applications
papers.abstract: Artificial intelligence has the potential to transform oncology across the continuum of care from prevention and screening to treatment and survivorship. This commentary discusses likely developments but presents no empirical analysis or model evaluation.
papers.methodology_hint: commentary, no model, no data, no evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: Commentary on AI potential across the oncology care continuum.

papers.title: Multi-Task Learning for Joint Prediction of Overall Survival and Treatment Response in Breast Cancer
papers.abstract: We train a multi-task neural network on a multi-omics dataset (mRNA, miRNA, methylation) from 1,100 TCGA breast cancer patients to jointly predict overall survival and pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy. C-index 0.71 for survival and AUC 0.82 for pCR are achieved. Sharing representations between tasks improves both versus single-task models. An external METABRIC cohort validates survival prediction.
papers.topic_hint: multi-task learning, breast cancer, overall survival, pCR, multi-omics, TCGA
papers.methodology_hint: multi-task NN, TCGA 1100 patients, C-index 0.71, AUC 0.82, single-task comparison, METABRIC validation
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-task NN for joint OS and pCR prediction in breast cancer, C-index 0.71, external validation.

papers.title: Transformer for Predicting Immunotherapy Response in NSCLC from Tumour Microenvironment Features
papers.abstract: PD-L1 status alone is an imperfect immunotherapy response predictor. We extract spatial tumour microenvironment features from multiplex immunofluorescence images and train a transformer encoder to predict durable response to anti-PD-1 therapy in 420 NSCLC patients. AUC 0.79 is achieved, substantially outperforming PD-L1 IHC and TMB. We do not compare against deep learning image baselines.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, NSCLC, immunotherapy response, tumour microenvironment, multiplex IF
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 420 NSCLC patients, AUC 0.79, PD-L1 and TMB comparison — no DL image baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for immunotherapy response prediction from TME features, AUC 0.79, outperforming PD-L1.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Automated Breast Cancer Detection in Mammography
papers.abstract: We develop a convolutional neural network for breast cancer detection in screening mammography, trained on 120,000 exams. AUC 0.94 is achieved, outperforming radiologist stand-alone reads in a reader study. This is a screening detection task; the model identifies cancer presence but does not predict survival or treatment response outcomes.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, mammography, breast cancer detection, screening, radiologist comparison
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 120k mammograms, AUC 0.94, reader study — screening detection not outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for breast cancer screening detection AUC 0.94 outperforming radiologists, not prognosis.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for 5-Year Colorectal Cancer Survival from Pathology Report Text
papers.abstract: Pathology reports contain prognostic information beyond stage. We build a GNN over concept graphs extracted from 3,800 colorectal cancer pathology reports and train to predict 5-year survival. C-index 0.74 is achieved. Only a logistic regression text baseline is compared; no Cox or survival-specific baselines are reported.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, colorectal cancer, pathology reports, 5-year survival, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: GNN on concept graphs, 3800 reports, C-index 0.74, only LR baseline — no Cox comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for 5-year colorectal survival from pathology text, C-index 0.74, limited baseline.

papers.title: Support Vector Machine for Prostate Cancer Screening Using PSA Velocity
papers.abstract: PSA velocity may improve prostate cancer screening specificity. We train an SVM on PSA trajectories from 4,200 men to classify cancer presence versus benign hypertrophy. Sensitivity 0.80 at specificity 0.75 is achieved. This is a screening and diagnostic classification task, not an outcome or survival prediction model.
papers.topic_hint: SVM, PSA, prostate cancer screening, classification
papers.methodology_hint: SVM, PSA velocity, sensitivity/specificity, cancer screening — diagnosis not outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: SVM for prostate cancer screening from PSA velocity, sensitivity 0.80, diagnostic not prognostic.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for Predicting Chemotherapy Toxicity Grade in Elderly Cancer Patients
papers.abstract: Elderly patients face high chemotherapy toxicity risk. We train a gradient boosting model on geriatric assessment scores, comorbidities, and baseline labs from 2,100 patients across four cancer types to predict grade 3+ toxicity within the first cycle. AUC 0.78 is achieved. Comparison against the CARG and CRASH toxicity scores is provided alongside an ablation of geriatric features.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, chemotherapy toxicity, elderly, geriatric oncology
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, 2100 patients, AUC 0.78, CARG/CRASH comparison, geriatric feature ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for chemotherapy toxicity prediction in elderly, AUC 0.78, clinical score comparison.

papers.title: Machine Learning in Oncology Drug Discovery: Accelerating Lead Identification
papers.abstract: We apply random forest and graph neural network models to predict cytotoxicity IC50 values for novel compound libraries screened against 12 cancer cell lines from the NCI-60 panel. The models guide compound prioritisation in a drug discovery campaign. This is a drug discovery application; patient survival outcomes are not modelled.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, GNN, cancer drug discovery, IC50, NCI-60, compound screening
papers.methodology_hint: RF and GNN, NCI-60, IC50 prediction — drug discovery not patient outcome prediction
papers.claimed_contribution: RF and GNN for cancer drug IC50 prediction in discovery campaign, patient outcomes not modelled.
